lexicon.title: Garden Lexicon (Exhaustive)
lexicon.description: Synonyms, keywords, entity hints, and expressive rules for mapping user text/imported notes into fixed gardening method IDs/keys used by SSA layer spine. Includes indigenous/culture-rooted and natural methods alongside modern techniques. Safety-first for chemicals, tools, and heat.
notes: This lexicon maps garden language → methodKey. Each methodKey should exist in garden.methods.json.
Rules support safer/correct selection based on crop/life-stage, environment, and constraints (heat, frost, chemicals).
Cultural methods are included as named approaches; SSA should present them respectfully and adapt them to local conditions/preferences via overrides.

rules.title: Frost/freeze cues → frost protection
rules.explain: When text mentions frost or freeze risk, prioritize frost protection and row cover actions.

actions.note: Cover before sunset if possible; remove or vent covers in the morning to prevent overheating.
Water early morning; use shade cloth during peak sun; avoid foliar sprays at midday.

rules.title: Noon spraying caution
rules.explain: Spraying oils/soaps in strong sun can burn leaves.

actions.warning: Avoid spraying neem/soap/oils in hot sun. Spray early morning or evening; test on a small section first.

actions.reason: Use sprays outdoors or with airflow.

rules.title: Pest cues → IPM monitoring first
rules.explain: If pests are mentioned, suggest scouting/monitoring and least-toxic approaches before broad sprays.

actions.note: Start with identification + monitoring (IPM). Use row cover or hand-picking when possible.
BTK targets caterpillars; apply in evening and reapply after rain per label directions.

rules.title: Seed cues with trays/lights → indoor seed starting

actions.note: Use sterile seed-starting mix; keep evenly moist; provide strong light close to seedlings to prevent legginess.

rules.title: Direct sow cues → direct sow method

actions.note: Mark rows, keep seedbed moist through germination, and thin seedlings for airflow.
Water seedlings before and after transplant. Plant at the right depth for the crop (e.g., tomatoes can be planted deeper).

rules.title: No-till / sheet mulch cues → no-till prep

actions.note: Overlap cardboard seams, soak thoroughly, and top with compost + mulch for best results.
Keep mulch a few inches away from plant stems; avoid piling against tree trunks.
Test before major amendments. Adjust gradually; over-correction can harm plants.

rules.title: Harvest cues → harvest and post-harvest handling

actions.note: Harvest in the cool of morning when possible; shade produce quickly to preserve freshness.
Curing needs warmth + airflow; avoid stacking too tightly and keep out of direct sun.

rules.title: Dryland/arid cues → zai pits / waffle gardens / swales

actions.note: In arid climates, prioritize water-harvesting earthworks + mulching to reduce evaporation.

rules.title: Guild/companions cues → companion planting and traditional polycultures

actions.note: Pair plants by needs: sun, water, nutrients, and growth habit. Leave airflow to reduce disease.
